name: JRiver Media Center
description: JRiver Media Center is a comprehensive media management application for audio, video, images, and television. This LXC runs MC35 on Debian 13 with browser-based noVNC access over HTTPS, while Media Server mode runs by default for more fault-tolerant daily use.

notes.text: A JRiver Media Center license is required. Purchase at https://www.jriver.com/
Open https://CONTAINER_IP:5800/setup/ to create the initial web credentials for the JRMC Dashboard and noVNC access. The HTTPS certificate is self-signed.
The container starts in Media Server mode. Use the Dashboard to launch the full JRMC UI only when interactive administration is needed.

Dashboard can optionally enable secure native VNC access on port 5902 while UI mode is active. Native VNC uses TLS-encrypted username/password authentication for Remmina, TigerVNC, and similar desktop clients, with the same username and password as the Dashboard, while browser noVNC keeps its separate resize-capable backend.
Run `jrmc-configure` for local administration, `jrmc-mode` to switch between Media Server and UI modes, and `jrmc-activate /path/to/file.mjr` to import a license restore file.

Installation uses the installJRMC community script (https://github.com/cryobry/installJRMC) for JRiver package installation and updates, while this LXC manages noVNC, web access, and service mode switching locally.
